## Screenshots

### Alert Queue — Risk-Ranked Triage

![Alert Queue Tab](data:image/png;base64,)

> Alerts ranked by **Breach Risk Score (BRS)** — not FIFO. Red = URGENT (BRS ≥ 75), Amber = WATCH, Green = NORMAL. SLA countdown turns red at < 30 minutes.

---

### Analytics — Performance Intelligence

![Analytics Tab](data:image/png;base64,)

> Hourly alert volume vs breach count · Alert type distribution donut · 7-day BRS trend · Analyst performance bar chart.

---

### Case Detail Sidebar — Decision Workflow

![Case Detail Sidebar](data:image/png;base64,)

> Single-pane case view with BRS gauge, customer data, and structured decision form. Mandatory rationale field (min 20 chars) enforces audit compliance.

---

## Project Name

| Token | Meaning |
|-------|---------|
| `sentinel` | A watchguard — monitors and flags threats *before* they breach |
| `aml` | Anti-Money Laundering — the compliance domain |
| `dashboard` | The deliverable: a live analyst-facing triage interface |

```
GitHub repo:   sentinel-aml-dashboard
Display name:  Sentinel AML Dashboard
Tagline:       Predict breaches before they happen — not after.
```

---

## The Problem This Solves

Financial crime compliance analysts receive hundreds of AML alerts daily. The legacy workflow has three critical failures:

| # | Failure | Impact |
|---|---------|--------|
| 1 | Alerts served **first-in, first-out** — no risk ranking | A case 5 minutes from SLA breach looks identical to one raised an hour ago |
| 2 | **No early-warning mechanism** | Analysts discover a breach has *occurred*, not that one is *approaching* |
| 3 | Case context lives in **4 separate systems** | 20–30 minutes of context-switching overhead per case |

This project redesigns that workflow end-to-end using a **Breach Risk Score (BRS)** model and a unified analyst dashboard.

---

## Repository Structure

```
sentinel-aml-dashboard/
│
├── README.md                         ← You are here
├── LICENSE
│
├── docs/
│   ├── BA_Requirements_Spec.docx     ← Full BA artefact (13 sections)
│   ├── as-is-process.md              ← Current state process map
│   ├── to-be-process.md              ← Redesigned workflow
│   ├── stakeholder-register.md       ← 6 stakeholders + conflict matrix
│   └── data-dictionary.md            ← 10-field data dictionary
│
└── dashboard/
    └── AML_Dashboard.jsx             ← React dashboard (single-file)
```

---

## BA Artefacts Included

> This is a **complete BA deliverable** — not just a UI. Every artefact maps to a real BA work product.

| Artefact | Location | BA Skill Demonstrated |
|----------|----------|-----------------------|
| Requirements Specification (13 sections) | `docs/BA_Requirements_Spec.docx` | Elicitation, MoSCoW prioritisation |
| As-Is Process Map (7-step, 4-system) | `docs/as-is-process.md` | Process analysis, Lean waste identification |
| To-Be Process Design | `docs/to-be-process.md` | Solution design, process improvement |
| Stakeholder Register + Conflict Matrix | `docs/stakeholder-register.md` | Stakeholder management |
| 12-item Functional Requirements Backlog | Section 6 of spec | User stories, acceptance criteria |
| 2 Structured Use Cases | Section 7 of spec | Use case modelling |
| Non-Functional Requirements (7 items) | Section 8 of spec | NFR definition |
| Risk Register (5 risks rated) | Section 9 of spec | Risk analysis and mitigation |
| 10-Field Data Dictionary | `docs/data-dictionary.md` | Data analysis, field-level spec |
| Gherkin Acceptance Criteria | Section 11 of spec | BDD, testable requirements |
| Requirements Traceability Matrix | Section 12 of spec | Requirements traceability |
| Live React Dashboard | `dashboard/AML_Dashboard.jsx` | Solution demonstration |

---

## Breach Risk Score (BRS) — Core Concept

Defined at **requirements level** — the BA specifies *what* it must produce, not *how* it is built internally.

```
Inputs
  ├── Time elapsed vs SLA window            weight: HIGH
  ├── Historical breach rate (alert type)   weight: MEDIUM
  ├── Case complexity (tx volume, 30d)      weight: MEDIUM
  └── Customer risk tier (LOW/MED/HIGH/PEP) weight: HIGH

Output
  ├── Normalised integer 0–100
  ├── 100 = breach certain within 30 minutes
  └── Refreshed every 5 minutes

Thresholds
  ├── BRS >= 75  →  URGENT   queue top, immediate action required
  ├── BRS 40–74  →  WATCH    monitor, approaching risk
  └── BRS < 40   →  NORMAL   standard processing
```

---

## Process Improvement Summary

| Metric | As-Is Baseline | To-Be Target | Lever |
|--------|---------------|--------------|-------|
| SLA breach rate | 12% | **< 4%** | Risk-ranked queue |
| Analyst triage time | 45 min/day | **< 10 min/day** | BRS automation |
| System switches per case | 4 systems | **1 unified view** | Single-pane dashboard |
| False positive review rate | 68% | **< 40%** | Confidence score threshold |
| Audit trail consistency | Manual, inconsistent | **100% automated** | System-generated log |

---

## Dashboard Features

### Tab 1 — Alert Queue
- Risk-ranked table sorted by BRS, not creation time
- Live BRS ticker — scores update every 3 seconds, queue reorders automatically
- Badge system: URGENT / WATCH / NORMAL
- SLA countdown per alert — turns red at < 30 minutes remaining
- Click any alert to open single-pane case detail sidebar
- Decision panel: CLEAR / ESCALATE / FILE SAR with mandatory rationale (validates min 20 chars)
- Search by customer name or alert ID; filter by status

### Tab 2 — Analytics
- Hourly alert volume vs SLA breach count (area chart)
- Alert type distribution donut (Structuring, Layering, Velocity, Cross-border, Round-tripping)
- 7-day average BRS trend line with value labels
- Analyst performance bar chart — cases closed vs SLA breached

### Tab 3 — Audit Log
- Immutable record of every closed case this session
- Captures: analyst ID, decision, BRS at closure, SLA remaining, rationale, UTC timestamp
- When empty: renders the full data dictionary specification inline

---

## Tech Stack

```
React 18        ·  component framework
Recharts        ·  AreaChart, BarChart, LineChart, PieChart, RadarChart
IBM Plex Mono   ·  typography (Google Fonts)
CSS-in-JS       ·  inline styles, zero external CSS dependencies
```

---

## Running Locally

```bash
git clone https://github.com/VijayKumaro7/sentinel-aml-dashboard.git
cd sentinel-aml-dashboard
npx create-react-app .
cp dashboard/AML_Dashboard.jsx src/App.jsx
npm install recharts
npm start
```

---

## Key Design Decisions

**Why BRS over a simple SLA countdown?**
A countdown tells you *when* a case will breach but not *which* cases are most dangerous. BRS combines urgency with case characteristics — a low-risk threshold breach should never rank above a complex structuring case on a PEP customer with 3 prior SARs.

**Why mandatory rationale field?**
Regulatory audit requirements demand every analyst decision be traceable. A dropdown alone creates no record of reasoning. The 20-character minimum forces structured thinking and produces defensible audit records.

**Why single-pane case view?**
Lean process analysis identified context-switching across 4 systems as the highest-impact waste category. Aggregating CRM, transaction history, and SAR data into one view eliminates ~25 minutes of daily overhead per analyst.

---

## Stakeholder Map

```
                      HIGH INFLUENCE
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │   Compliance Manager ──────── MLRO       │
    │   Financial Crime Tech Lead              │
    │   Risk & Audit Team                      │
    └──────────────────────────────────────────┘
                      LOW INFLUENCE
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │   AML Analyst            Data Engineering│
    │   (HIGH interest)        (LOW interest)  │
    └──────────────────────────────────────────┘

Key conflict resolved
  Analyst wants fewer alerts  vs  Manager wants zero false negatives
  Solution: configurable BRS sensitivity threshold
```
